name: Suite — Coverage Supplement (gap-filler for comprehensive suite)
description: Hand-curated supplement to the comprehensive suite. The audit (2026-05-24) found 79% of comprehensive scenarios lack ExpectedSql and several shape categories are barely covered: bracket grammar (2 of 231), window functions (0), recursive CTE / hierarchies (2), tool dispatch (0), semantic search (6), explicit refusals (5). This suite adds ~40 realistic scenarios across those gaps, all with ExpectedSql so the EX-accuracy checker can grade them. Designed to be the SECOND suite to run after the comprehensive one — gives the baseline measurement coverage of the routing branches and shapes the main suite misses.

Scenarios.Question: show me users [Id, UserName] and their role
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.Id, u.UserName, r.Name AS RoleName FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON ur.UserId = u.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId
Scenarios._note: The user-reported bracket failure. Tests StructuralCueParser + JOIN.

Scenarios.Question: users (Id, UserName) and their role
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.Id, u.UserName, r.Name AS RoleName FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON ur.UserId = u.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId

Scenarios.Question: tickets - TicketNumber - Title - status
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS Status FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: اعرض المستخدمين [الرقم، الاسم] والدور
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.Id, u.UserName, r.Name AS RoleName FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON ur.UserId = u.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId

Scenarios.Question: show users with Id, UserName and role
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.Id, u.UserName, r.Name AS RoleName FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON ur.UserId = u.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId
Scenarios._note: Same intent, no brackets — control case.

Scenarios.Question: rank departments by ticket count
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT d.NameEn, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(t.Id) DESC) AS Rank FROM Departments d LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.DepartmentId = d.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY d.NameEn
Scenarios._note: Window function — RANK() with LEFT JOIN to include zero-ticket departments.

Scenarios.Question: top 3 tickets per priority
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TicketNumber, Title, Priority FROM (SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS Priority, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC) AS rn FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0) x WHERE rn <= 3
Scenarios._note: ROW_NUMBER() with PARTITION BY — classic top-N-per-group.

Scenarios.Question: running total of bills by month
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT FORMAT(IssueDate, 'yyyy-MM') AS Month, SUM(TotalAmount) AS MonthTotal, SUM(SUM(TotalAmount)) OVER (ORDER BY FORMAT(IssueDate, 'yyyy-MM')) AS RunningTotal FROM Bills GROUP BY FORMAT(IssueDate, 'yyyy-MM') ORDER BY Month
Scenarios._note: Window over aggregate — running total pattern.

Scenarios.Question: rank customers by total billed amount
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT c.FullNameEn, SUM(b.TotalAmount) AS Total, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(b.TotalAmount) DESC) AS Rank FROM Customers c JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId = c.Id GROUP BY c.Id, c.FullNameEn

Scenarios.Question: ticket count change month over month
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') AS Month, COUNT(*) AS [Count], COUNT(*) - LAG(COUNT(*), 1) OVER (ORDER BY FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM')) AS DeltaFromPrevMonth FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') ORDER BY Month

Scenarios.Question: all subcategories under each top-level ticket category
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: WITH Recursive AS (SELECT Id, Name, ParentCategoryId, CAST(Name AS NVARCHAR(MAX)) AS Path FROM TicketCategories WHERE ParentCategoryId IS NULL UNION ALL SELECT c.Id, c.Name, c.ParentCategoryId, CAST(r.Path + ' > ' + c.Name AS NVARCHAR(MAX)) FROM TicketCategories c JOIN Recursive r ON c.ParentCategoryId = r.Id) SELECT Path FROM Recursive WHERE ParentCategoryId IS NOT NULL ORDER BY Path
Scenarios._note: Recursive CTE — hierarchical traversal of TicketCategories.

Scenarios.Question: departments and their ticket totals broken down by status
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: WITH PerStatus AS (SELECT d.NameEn AS Dept, s.Name AS Status, COUNT(*) AS Cnt FROM Departments d JOIN Tickets t ON t.DepartmentId = d.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId GROUP BY d.NameEn, s.Name) SELECT Dept, Status, Cnt FROM PerStatus ORDER BY Dept, Cnt DESC

Scenarios.Question: regions with their districts
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: WITH RegionTree AS (SELECT Id, NameEn, ParentRegionId, 0 AS Lvl FROM Regions WHERE ParentRegionId IS NULL UNION ALL SELECT r.Id, r.NameEn, r.ParentRegionId, rt.Lvl + 1 FROM Regions r JOIN RegionTree rt ON r.ParentRegionId = rt.Id) SELECT NameEn, Lvl FROM RegionTree ORDER BY Lvl, NameEn

Scenarios.Question: what is the weather in Damascus today
Scenarios._note: Tool dispatch test — should route to external weather tool, not generate SQL.

Scenarios.Question: what's the temperature in Aleppo right now
is today a public holiday
Scenarios._note: Holiday-lookup tool if registered; otherwise should refuse cleanly.

Scenarios.Question: tickets about login errors
Scenarios._note: Free-text semantic search over ticket title/description. Verifies the semantic-search handler fires.

Scenarios.Question: delete all tickets
Scenarios._note: Write-intent refusal — caught by WriteIntentGuard before any LLM call.

Scenarios.Question: predict next month's ticket volume
Scenarios._note: Genuine future forecast — refuse. NOTE: 'why did X spike' is analytical (data_query), this is forecasting (refuse).

Scenarios.Question: which department is the prettiest
Scenarios._note: Pure subjective preference — no data answer.

Scenarios.Question: insert a new admin user with password 'hunter2'
hi there
Scenarios._note: Conversational fast-path — must NOT hit LLM SQL path.

Scenarios.Question: what tables exist in the database
Scenarios._note: Metadata query — schema description, not data.

Scenarios.Question: what columns does the Tickets table have
tickets in damascus
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN Customers c ON c.Id = t.CustomerId JOIN Regions r ON r.Id = c.RegionId WHERE r.NameEn = 'Damascus' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
Scenarios._note: Entity resolution test — 'damascus' (lowercase) should resolve to Regions.NameEn = 'Damascus' via fuzzy match.

Scenarios.Question: outages in aleppo last week
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM Outages o JOIN Regions r ON r.Id = o.RegionId WHERE r.NameEn = 'Aleppo' AND o.StartedAt >= DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE())

Scenarios.Question: customers in homs with overdue bills
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT c.FullNameEn, c.Phone FROM Customers c JOIN Regions r ON r.Id = c.RegionId JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId = c.Id WHERE r.NameEn = 'Homs' AND b.Status = 'Overdue'
